roadmap.revision: REV-3 (design-fidelity fix — the missing FrontendAddrAllocator WRITER). REV-2 wired every READER of the FrontendAddrAllocator (01-03 name_index, 02-00 by_frontend) and the single-owner injection (02-01) but NAMED NO production actor that calls assign(<job>) on <job> declaration. The just-landed 01-03 (commit 53236c6a) made the DNS read path frontend_for the de-facto writer (name_index.rs:262 calls allocator.assign on every query) — order-dependent, non-deterministic, and turning the DNS index into a second writer to a shared allocator. ROOT CAUSE: ADR-0072 1a-A / feature-delta § Frontend lifecycle contract describe the binding as happening passively ('bound on first running-AND-healthy backend', 'the drain looks up / binds <job> -> F') but name no actor + no call site; the feature-delta C4 even places the WRITE in the health-gated ordered drain, which contradicts Finding-2 (the binding must be ORTHOGONAL to health, surviving zero-healthy windows). REV-3 adds 01-05 (NEW — the deploy-time lifecycle assigner: assign(<job>) at admission in the POST /v1/jobs Service-arm handler, mirroring the ServiceVipAllocator admission-time allocate at handlers.rs:324; empty-on-boot Bar-1 converge-on-boot rebuild from declared-Service intent before the convergence loop, mirroring NetSlotAllocator adopt_on_restart_recovery at lib.rs:1980); re-scopes 01-03 (name_index frontend_for is now a PURE READER — read-only lookup of the allocator's EXISTING binding, NEVER assign-on-read); EXTENDS 02-01 (the assigner is wired/injected at the same composition root, single-owner invariant intact). Mints S-DBN-ASSIGN-01..04. NOTE: release(<job>) has NO production trigger today — there is no logical-workload-DELETION verb (router has only POST /v1/jobs, POST /v1/jobs/:id/stop=transient, GET reads — lib.rs:2094-2100); see 01-05 BLOCKER note + the orchestrator return. 43 S-DBN-* scenarios (was 39). REV-2 base unchanged below: REV-2 adds 01-04 (FrontendAddrAllocator) + 02-00 (MtlsResolve re-key); re-scopes 01-03/02-01/02-02/03-01; 01-01/01-02/03-02 unchanged.

steps.name: MeshServiceName newtype — the <job>.svc.overdrive.local grammar
criteria: S-DBN-NAME-01: a mandatory proptest round-trip in crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/core_newtype_roundtrip.rs proves that for every valid <job> label L (1..=LABEL_MAX after the fixed '.svc.overdrive.local' suffix budget), a MeshServiceName built from '<L>.svc.overdrive.local' survives Display->FromStr (re-parse equals original), as_str() yields the canonical lowercase <job> label, and serde_json::to_string/from_str round-trips to the quoted Display form (serde matches Display/FromStr exactly, the mandatory newtype rule per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'Newtype roundtrip'). proptest is the paradigm here, not single #[test].

S-DBN-NAME-02: a proptest in core_newtype_roundtrip.rs proves FromStr/new is case-insensitive — for every case-permutation P of '<L>.svc.overdrive.local' (mixed case in BOTH label and suffix), MeshServiceName::new(P) succeeds and equals new of the all-lowercase form, and Display emits the lowercase canonical (matches the validate_label precedent at id.rs:99).

S-DBN-NAME-03: an example-matrix #[test] in crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/core_newtype_validation.rs proves the bespoke suffix FromStr accepts 'server.svc.overdrive.local' (<job>=server) and 'payments-api.svc.overdrive.local', and rejects wrong suffix ('server.svc.example.com'), non-terminal suffix ('server.svc.overdrive.local.evil'), missing suffix ('server'), missing .svc segment ('server.overdrive.local'), and empty <job> ('.svc.overdrive.local') — each rejection returning Err(IdParseError::<variant>); the per-case variant is a DELIVER refinement, the test asserts is_err()+IdParseError and pins the accepted-case <job> extraction.

S-DBN-NAME-04: a proptest in core_newtype_validation.rs proves every label violating DNS-1123 rules (empty, > LABEL_MAX after suffix budget, leading/trailing non-alphanumeric, out-of-class char) returns Err(IdParseError::<variant>) — never panics, never silently truncates; the ceiling is sized off overdrive_core::id::LABEL_MAX (253), never a bespoke smaller magic number (development.md § 'One shared length ceiling').

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design'): MeshServiceName lands in crates/overdrive-core/src/id.rs with const SUFFIX = "svc.overdrive.local", new(&str) -> Result<Self, IdParseError>, as_str(&self) -> &str, + Display (lowercase) + FromStr (case-insensitive) + Serialize/Deserialize (matching) + TryFrom<String>/<&str>; modeled on define_label_newtype! + validate_label + LABEL_MAX with a bespoke suffix FromStr. Invent NO public surface beyond this.

steps.name: NameAnswer enum + hickory-proto wire codec (encode/decode)
criteria: S-DBN-WIRE-01: a proptest (symmetric round-trip) in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/dns_wire.rs proves that for every query name N and non-empty SocketAddrV4 set, wire::encode(N, A, NameAnswer::Records(addrs)) -> bytes decoded by hickory_proto's Message parser yields ResponseCode::NoError, exactly one A record per addr, the decoded A-record set equals the input set, and ANCOUNT == addrs.len(). proptest is the paradigm.

S-DBN-WIRE-02: a proptest in dns_wire.rs proves wire::encode(N, AAAA, NameAnswer::NoData) -> bytes decoded by hickory yields ResponseCode::NoError, ANCOUNT == 0, exactly one SOA in the AUTHORITY section, and that SOA's MINIMUM (RFC 2308 negative TTL) == 1 (DDN-8).

S-DBN-WIRE-03: a proptest in dns_wire.rs proves that for every name N and qtype Q in {A, AAAA}, wire::encode(N, Q, NameAnswer::NxDomain) -> bytes decoded by hickory yields ResponseCode::NXDomain, ANCOUNT == 0, and exactly one SOA in AUTHORITY with MINIMUM == 1 (the negative-TTL SOA so the stub resolver re-resolves once a backend is running-and-healthy).

S-DBN-WIRE-04: a proptest in dns_wire.rs proves the SOA SERIAL is a deterministic function of an injected Arc<dyn Clock> reading T — two encodes with the SAME T produce byte-identical SOA SERIAL, two encodes with DISTINCT T (differing past the SERIAL granularity, pinned in the @example per the DISTILL reviewer suggestion #3) produce distinct SERIALs; the encoder NEVER reads wall-clock (development.md § 'Never call SystemTime::now() in core logic').

Pinned surface ONLY: enum NameAnswer { Records(Vec<SocketAddrV4>), NoData, NxDomain } lands in overdrive-core (id.rs or a small dns module — DESIGN's own intentional latitude per ADR-0072 § Components, DELIVER picks one and documents it, inventing no surface); the variant names ARE the contract. NameAnswer stays hickory-free; only the qtype (hickory_proto::rr::RecordType) crosses the wire.rs ACL boundary. The wire codec lands in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dns_responder/wire.rs as hickory-proto decode (query) + encode (A / NODATA-SOA / NXDOMAIN-SOA, MINIMUM=1, SERIAL via Clock).

Wiring step (DELIVER, per ADR-0072 § Components): ADD hickory-proto (Apache-2.0/MIT) to root Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies] and pull it into overdrive-control-plane — this is why these scaffolds could not land mid-DISTILL. The dns_responder/mod.rs module wiring + re-exports land here.

steps.name: FrontendAddrAllocator — stable per-<job> frontend addr in 10.98.0.0/16 (1a-A)
criteria: S-DBN-FRONTEND-01: a proptest in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/dns_frontend_allocator.rs proves that for every <job> label J, a fresh FrontendAddrAllocator's assign(J) returns Ok(F) where F is an Ipv4Addr AND F is a member of WORKLOAD_FRONTEND_BASE (10.98.0.0/16) AND F is disjoint from WORKLOAD_SUBNET_BASE (10.99.0.0/16, the per-netns /30s at veth_provisioner.rs:307) AND F is disjoint from the service-VIP range (10.96.0.0/16, VipRange::default()) — the membership is asserted against the two REAL named consts, never a magic number (this is the collision the spike's 10.96.0.0/16 candidate was REJECTED for, ADR-0072 § Collision check). @example: J=server, F is the allocator's first stable assignment in 10.98.0.0/16. proptest is the paradigm.

S-DBN-FRONTEND-02: a proptest in dns_frontend_allocator.rs proves the addr is RETAINED across an alloc cycle — for every <job> J and any sequence of intervening assigns of OTHER <job>s, once assign(J) returned F, a second assign(J) (the alloc-cycle case: stop -> new AllocationId -> new workload_addr but the SAME logical <job>) returns the SAME F (idempotent per <job>), and F is unchanged regardless of intervening assigns/releases of other <job>s. The atomic per-<job> claim follows development.md § 'Check-and-act must be atomic' (the assign return IS the claim). This is the SQ1-elimination contract at the allocator layer.

S-DBN-FRONTEND-03 (withhold-not-release, Finding-2): a proptest in dns_frontend_allocator.rs proves the addr is RELEASED ONLY on logical-workload deletion, NEVER on a transient zero-healthy state — PROPERTY 1: given assign(J) returned F, while release(J) is NOT called (the transient zero-healthy window — the <job> is still declared, the allocator is never told to release), a subsequent assign(J) STILL returns the SAME F AND snapshot() still contains J -> F (the allocator carries NO health state). PROPERTY 2 (the genuine end): when release(J) IS called (logical-workload deletion/undeploy), snapshot() no longer contains J AND a later assign of a DIFFERENT <job> MAY draw F (the binding genuinely ended). Releasing F on a transient zero-healthy state would reintroduce the SQ1 stale-cached-F failure — the explicitly-rejected path.

S-DBN-FRONTEND-04: a proptest in dns_frontend_allocator.rs proves collision-free distinct assignment — for every set of distinct <job> labels {J1..Jn} (n within the 10.98.0.0/16 block capacity), each Ji assign()ed yields pairwise-distinct {F1..Fn} (no collision — the rejected 1a-C deterministic-hash birthday-bound risk; 1a-A is an allocator precisely to avoid it), each Fi a member of 10.98.0.0/16, AND after release(Jk) a fresh assign of a NEW <job> MAY reuse Fk (the block is reclaimed; drop-then-reassign tracks the live set, the NetSlotAllocator converge-on-boot rebuild discipline).

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design'): pub struct FrontendAddrAllocator (a per-host <job> -> Ipv4Addr map, Arc<Mutex<BTreeMap>>, boot-rebuilt empty-on-boot per the NetSlotAllocator precedent) lands in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dns_responder/frontend_addr_allocator.rs with new() -> Self; assign(&self, job: &WorkloadId /* or MeshServiceName */) -> Result<Ipv4Addr, FrontendAddrExhausted> (idempotent per <job>, atomic check-and-act); release(&self, job) (logical-workload-DELETION only — NOT alloc cycle, NOT transient zero-healthy; zero-healthy is the name_index's WITHHOLD seam in 01-03, NOT a release here); snapshot() -> BTreeMap<job, Ipv4Addr>; const WORKLOAD_FRONTEND_BASE = 10.98.0.0/16 (Ipv4Net, lexically distinct from the existing ServiceFrontend VIP type at dataplane/service_frontend.rs). The exact slot-within-block arithmetic + ceiling is a DELIVER detail (mirror NetSlotAllocator's pure smallest-free scan); the block + the public surface above are the contract. Invent NO public surface beyond this. BLOCKER-1 RESOLVED (spike WORKS — the 10.98.0.0/16 block is routable+capturable through the shipped Path-A datapath); no dataplane gate remains on this step.

Mutation gate: a per-step diff-scoped run — cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dns_responder/frontend_addr_allocator.rs — achieves kill-rate >= 80%, targeting the pure smallest-free scan (a mutant that does not record the claim hands two <job>s the same F, flipping FRONTEND-04 pairwise-distinctness), the idempotent-assign branch (a mutant that re-allocates on the second assign flips FRONTEND-02), and the release path (a mutant that releases on a non-deletion signal flips FRONTEND-03).

steps.name: NameIndex (<job> -> stable frontend addr F, healthy gate as WITHHOLD seam, OQ-1 pinned) + pure answer_for
criteria: S-DBN-ANSWER-01 (THE primary mutation-gate target, DDN-4; RE-SCOPED to stable F): a proptest in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/dns_answer_for.rs proves that for every RESOLVABLE <job> name N (>=1 running-AND-healthy backend, so the name_index holds N -> F where F is N's STABLE frontend addr — REV-2: NOT a backend-addr set) plus arbitrary other resolvable names, answer_for(N, RecordType::A, &index) returns NameAnswer::Records(addrs) where addrs == vec![F] (exactly the single stable frontend addr, never a per-instance backend addr in 10.99.0.0/16). @example: N=server, F=10.98.x.y. Single-stable-F equality is the fail-closed guard.

S-DBN-ANSWER-02 (RE-SCOPED): a proptest in dns_answer_for.rs proves that for every name N absent from the index OR present with 0 running-and-healthy backends, answer_for(N, RecordType::A, &index) returns NameAnswer::NxDomain and NEVER Records (the answer is WITHHELD — no stale/cached/guessed/frontend addr; declared-but-not-running, all-unhealthy, and unknown all collapse to NxDomain in v1).

S-DBN-ANSWER-03 (RE-SCOPED): a proptest in dns_answer_for.rs proves answer_for(N, RecordType::AAAA, &index) returns NameAnswer::NoData for a resolvable name and NameAnswer::NxDomain for a withheld/absent name — never a fabricated v6 addr (pins the v1 contract table's AAAA column).

S-DBN-ANSWER-04 (the load-bearing healthy gate as the WITHHOLD seam, REV-2 DDN-2/Finding-2): a proptest in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/dns_name_index.rs builds a NameIndex via the List-then-Watch path from rows where Backend.healthy == false and proves answer_for(N, A) -> NxDomain for the unhealthy-only name N (the gate governs *resolvability* — withhold the answer), while a co-resident name M with a running-AND-healthy backend resolves to its stable F (Records == vec![F_M]) — proving the gate withheld only N, not the index. Asserts on answer_for return, NEVER the by_name BTreeMap internals.

S-DBN-ANSWER-05 (PRESERVED): a single-example #[test] in dns_answer_for.rs proves answer_for('nonexistent.svc.overdrive.local', A, &index) -> NxDomain while 'server' in the same index still returns Records(vec![F]) (the miss does not corrupt the hit path).

S-DBN-IDX-01 (RE-SCOPED to stable F): a proptest in dns_name_index.rs (driven by SimObservationStore) proves the index seeds from List at probe — a running-and-healthy ServiceBackendRow R whose Backend.alloc SVID job segment == N's <job>, with the FrontendAddrAllocator binding N -> F, makes N resolve to its STABLE frontend addr F (Records == vec![F], NOT R's per-instance Backend.addr); AND the watch half: an index probed against an empty store becomes resolvable for N to its F once a running-and-healthy row for N written AFTER probe is processed by the watch drain. Validates the OQ-1 mapping consumer (rows grouped by <job> to decide resolvability, then expose the allocator's stable F).

S-DBN-IDX-02 (RE-SCOPED — withhold-not-release on the watch path, Finding-2): a proptest in dns_name_index.rs proves a name N initially resolvable to its stable F stops resolving (answer_for -> NxDomain) once a fresh row for the same backend with Backend.healthy == false is processed by the watch drain (the name_index WITHHOLDS), AND the FrontendAddrAllocator STILL binds N -> the SAME F (snapshot() still contains N -> F — withhold-not-release; the stable F is retained across the zero-healthy window), AND the moment a running-AND-healthy row for N is written again, N resolves to the SAME F (no churn of the addr). A mutant that releases F on zero-healthy passes the NXDOMAIN check but fails the retained-F check.

S-DBN-IDX-03 (PRESERVED, addr-agnostic): a proptest in dns_name_index.rs proves a SubscriptionEvent::Lagged { missed } triggers a re-List via all_service_backends_rows(), after which the index reflects the store state S exactly (every <job> with a running-and-healthy row in S resolves to its stable F, no <job> absent from S resolves) — the relist-on-Lagged recovery the ServiceBackendsResolve sibling enforces (re-derives resolvability from rows regardless of whether the exposed value is a backend addr or a stable F).

S-DBN-IDX-04 (RE-SCOPED — single source of frontend truth = the FrontendAddrAllocator, the SAME instance 02-00's by_frontend reads; frontend_for is a PURE READER, REV-3): a proptest in dns_name_index.rs proves the answered F is the FrontendAddrAllocator's binding and the index introduces no second source of frontend truth AND DOES NOT MUTATE the allocator on a read — for any resolvable N whose <job> the allocator ALREADY binds to F (the binding written by the 01-05 deploy-time assigner, NOT by this index), answer_for(N, A, &index) returns Records(vec![F]) (no fabricated addr, no cached snapshot that outlived the allocator state), AND removing all rows for N from S and re-deriving makes N resolve to NxDomain (withheld — no stale retention at the index) WHILE the FrontendAddrAllocator still binds N -> F (the binding is the allocator's, not the index's). CRITICAL (REV-3, the root-cause fix): frontend_for is a READ-ONLY lookup of the allocator's EXISTING <job> -> F binding — it MUST NOT call assign (the just-landed name_index.rs:262 `self.allocator.assign(name).ok()` is the de-facto-writer DEFECT this re-scope corrects). A query for a resolvable <job> that the allocator does NOT YET bind (the assigner has not run for it) is WITHHELD (NxDomain), NOT silently assigned-on-read: the assertion is that issuing a DNS query for an UN-ASSIGNED <job> leaves allocator.snapshot() UNCHANGED (no new binding appears as a side effect of the query) — a mutant that assigns-on-read adds a binding and flips this. The single source named here is byte-identical to the one 02-00's by_frontend re-key derives F from (the SAME FrontendAddrAllocator instance, injected once by run_server per 02-01 / ADR-0072 DDN-2), and the WRITER of that binding is the 01-05 deploy-time assigner — so the answered F == the recognized F and neither 01-03 nor 02-00 may introduce a second <job> -> F source NOR write the binding. The in-memory half of the K-DBN-4 single-source oracle (REV-2 form: two single-source claims — frontend truth = the FrontendAddrAllocator written by the 01-05 assigner, liveness truth = the service_backends rows; the index fabricates neither and writes neither).

OQ-1 STILL OPEN under REV-2 (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design — never invent API surface'): the crafter MUST surface OQ-1 — the SpiffeId -> <job> accessor, CONFIRMED-ABSENT today (SpiffeId exposes only as_str/trust_domain/path + for_allocation, id.rs:269-304) — needed now to key the FrontendAddrAllocator by <job> AND group running-and-healthy rows under it; pin the exact accessor signature (a new SpiffeId::job_segment() -> Option<&str> on the newtype OR a parse helper local to name_index.rs) as a DECISION before improvising; if the shape is ambiguous, return a BLOCKER. DISTILL picks no signature; the crafter must not reach for the nearest mechanism that compiles. (The FrontendKey newtype-vs-tuple shape and the CIDR-membership accessor are 02-00 DELIVER-open details, not this step.)

Pinned surface ONLY: pub fn answer_for(name: &MeshServiceName, qtype: hickory_proto::rr::RecordType, index: &NameIndex) -> NameAnswer (signature UNCHANGED; Records now holds vec![F], one stable frontend addr per <job>) lands in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dns_responder/answer.rs; NameIndex (maps <job> -> stable frontend addr F, NOT -> a backend-addr set) with List-then-Watch + relist-on-Lagged + single-owner-drain + probe() (mirroring ServiceBackendsResolve at mtls_resolve_adapter.rs:307-471), the mandatory Backend.healthy == true gate as the WITHHOLD seam (REV-2 DDN-2/Finding-2 — governs resolvability, not which addr), and READING (READ-ONLY) the FrontendAddrAllocator <job> -> F binding from the SAME FrontendAddrAllocator instance 02-00's by_frontend re-key reads (the dependency on 01-04 for the surface, on 01-05 for the WRITER that populates the binding; the single source of frontend truth, ADR-0072 DDN-2 single-owner invariant — run_server injects one instance into all of {writer 01-05, name_index, by_frontend} per 02-01), lands in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dns_responder/name_index.rs. frontend_for MUST be a PURE READER of the allocator's EXISTING binding — a read-only lookup (e.g. allocator.snapshot().get(<job>) or an allocator read accessor), NEVER allocator.assign on the query/read path (REV-3 root-cause fix: the de-facto-writer defect at name_index.rs:262 `self.allocator.assign(name).ok()` — where a DNS QUERY allocates a stable frontend addr as a side effect — is CORRECTED here; the allocator is WRITTEN only by the 01-05 deploy-time assigner at <job> declaration, never by a reader). The name_index MUST NOT introduce a second <job> -> F source AND MUST NOT write the binding (no fabricated addr, no cached snapshot, no assign-on-read) — the answered F is the allocator's binding (written by 01-05), byte-identical to the F by_frontend recognizes; a <job> the allocator does not yet bind is WITHHELD (NxDomain), not assigned-on-read. Invent NO public surface beyond this; the internal by_name structure is a DELIVER detail asserted only through answer_for. NOTE for the crafter: if the FrontendAddrAllocator (01-04) exposes only assign/release/snapshot today and no read-only single-<job> accessor, use snapshot().get(<job>) for the read (snapshot is the read-only surface) OR pin a read-only get accessor as a DECISION — do NOT reach for assign to read.

Mutation gate (answer_for is THE primary kill target, DDN-4): a per-step diff-scoped run — cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dns_responder/answer.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dns_responder/name_index.rs — achieves kill-rate >= 80%, targeting answer_for's Records(vec![F])/NxDomain/NoData arms (an empty/withheld/wrong-name mutant flips the single-stable-F equality) and the NameIndex healthy-gate WITHHOLD seam (a mutant dropping the filter resolves a should-be-withheld <job>; a mutant releasing F on zero-healthy flips the retained-F check IDX-02).

steps.name: FrontendAddrAllocator WRITER — deploy-time lifecycle assigner: assign(<job>) at <job> declaration + empty-on-boot converge-on-boot rebuild from declared-Service intent (REV-3, the missing writer)
criteria: S-DBN-ASSIGN-01 (assign-on-declare, the WRITER seam, REV-3 root-cause fix): a #[tokio::test] in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/dns_frontend_assigner.rs (driven by the in-process submit_workload handler path against a LocalIntentStore + a shared FrontendAddrAllocator on AppState, mirroring the S-VIP-* ServiceVipAllocator admission tests) proves that submitting a Service workload with [service].id = J through POST /v1/jobs causes FrontendAddrAllocator::assign(J)'s binding to be present — after the submit returns Ok, allocator.snapshot() contains <job>(J) -> F where F is a member of WORKLOAD_FRONTEND_BASE (10.98.0.0/16). The assign happens at admission in the Service arm of submit_workload (handlers.rs ~324, the SAME guard as the service_vip allocate `if matches!(intent, WorkloadIntent::Service(_))`), NOT on a DNS query and NOT in the name_index. A Job-kind (non-Service) submit does NOT assign a frontend addr (frontends are a Service-name concern, mirroring the VIP allocate's Service-only guard). @example: J=server, the submit binds server -> the first F in 10.98.0.0/16. This is the production call site the name_index's frontend_for read path MUST NOT substitute for (the 01-03 re-scope).

S-DBN-ASSIGN-02 (idempotent re-declare across resubmit, ties to FRONTEND-02): a #[tokio::test] in dns_frontend_assigner.rs proves a byte-identical resubmit of Service J (the KeyExists idempotency path, handlers.rs put_if_absent) does NOT consume a second frontend addr and does NOT change J's binding — after the first submit bound J -> F, a second submit of the SAME spec leaves allocator.snapshot()[J] == F unchanged (the assign is idempotent per <job>, FRONTEND-02 at the allocator layer; the handler's idempotency path must reach the SAME idempotent assign, never a fresh allocate). Pairs with the conflict-rollback shape: a CONFLICTING resubmit (a different spec at the same WorkloadId key, handlers.rs Conflict branch) MUST NOT mutate the existing J -> F binding (no eviction of the live workload's frontend on a rejected resubmit — mirrors the VIP release-on-conflict-ONLY discipline at handlers.rs:451-459, and the listener_facts U6 'do not evict the existing workload on a conflicting resubmit' guard at handlers.rs:466).

S-DBN-ASSIGN-03 (converge-on-boot rebuild — empty-on-boot, Bar-1, NetSlotAllocator precedent): a #[tokio::test] in dns_frontend_assigner.rs proves the boot rebuild re-populates the empty-on-boot allocator from the currently-DECLARED Service set BEFORE the convergence loop / responder serve spawn — GIVEN a LocalIntentStore already holding N declared Service intents (IntentKey::for_workload rows, as a prior boot left them) and a FRESH (empty) FrontendAddrAllocator, WHEN the boot rebuild pass runs (the dedicated converge-on-boot pass that enumerates declared-Service intents and idempotently assigns each <job>, mirroring veth_provisioner::adopt_on_restart_recovery at lib.rs:1980 — runs AFTER AppState so the shared allocator is available, BEFORE the convergence-loop / DnsResponder serve spawn so the first answered F is never an un-rebuilt miss), THEN allocator.snapshot() contains a stable F for every declared <job> AND re-running the rebuild is idempotent (re-assign of an already-held <job> returns the SAME F, no churn — the empty-on-boot rebuild discipline, reconcilers.md Bar-1 converge-on-boot). The allocator carries NO cross-restart persistence (it is NOT the persistent ServiceVipAllocator model — it is the ephemeral NetSlotAllocator model, ADR-0072 1a-A); the declared-Service intent IS the SSOT the rebuild re-derives from (development.md § 'Persist inputs, not derived state' — desired is the declared intent, never inferred from a prior allocator dump).

S-DBN-ASSIGN-04 (single-owner: the WRITER feeds the SAME instance the readers read, DDN-2): a #[tokio::test] in dns_frontend_assigner.rs proves the assigner writes the ONE FrontendAddrAllocator instance that the name_index (01-03) and the re-keyed MtlsResolve by_frontend (02-00) read — GIVEN the single Arc-shared FrontendAddrAllocator constructed by run_server (02-01) and injected into BOTH readers AND the writer path, WHEN the assigner binds <job> J -> F, THEN a name_index built over that SAME instance answers F for J once J has a running-AND-healthy backend (the answered F == the assigned F, byte-identical) AND the by_frontend key the ordered drain derives for J is keyed on that SAME F (no second <job> -> F source anywhere — neither the writer, the name_index, nor by_frontend fabricates an F; all three read the one allocator). A mutant that constructs a second allocator for the writer (or lets the name_index assign on read) makes the answered F miss by_frontend -> fail-closed, flipping this. The byte-consistency anchor of the REV-2 single-owner invariant (ADR-0072 DDN-2), now with the writer named.

OQ-1 SHARED with 01-03 (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design — never invent API surface'): the assigner needs the <job> key in MeshServiceName / WorkloadId form to call assign(<job>). The Service arm of submit_workload already holds the WorkloadId (handlers.rs ~281-285, `WorkloadIntent::Service(s) => s.id.clone()`), and FrontendAddrAllocator::assign is keyed by MeshServiceName today (the just-landed allocator, frontend_addr_allocator.rs:201). The crafter MUST pin the key-conversion decision (WorkloadId -> MeshServiceName via MeshServiceName::new(format!("{id}.{SUFFIX}")) at the assign call site, OR an assign overload keyed by WorkloadId) as a DECISION before improvising; this is the SAME OQ-1 surface 01-03 carries (the SpiffeId -> <job> direction is the name_index's row-grouping concern; here it is the WorkloadId -> MeshServiceName direction). Do NOT invent a new allocator method beyond the pinned assign/release/snapshot surface (01-04); if the key shape is ambiguous, return a BLOCKER.

BLOCKER (release has no production trigger — surface, do not improvise): release(<job>) on logical-workload DELETION is the symmetric half of the lifecycle (ADR-0072 1a-A / Finding-2; FRONTEND-03 Property 2 at Tier 1). VERIFIED: there is NO logical-workload-deletion verb in production today — the router exposes only POST /v1/jobs (declare), POST /v1/jobs/:id/stop (TRANSIENT — the IntentKey::for_workload row PERSISTS, the <job> stays declared; handlers.rs:718-764), and GET reads (lib.rs:2094-2100). 'stop' is the withhold-not-release case (S-DBN-FRONTEND-03 Property 1 / IDX-02 / NXDOMAIN-02), NOT a release. The ServiceVipAllocator precedent confirms this: the VIP is released ONLY on the conflict-rollback path (handlers.rs:451-459), NEVER on stop. THEREFORE 01-05 wires the assign half ONLY; release(<job>) is exercised at Tier 1 by FRONTEND-03 Property 2 (the allocator's release surface IS implemented and tested at 01-04) but has NO production CALL SITE to wire because the deletion edge does not exist. The crafter MUST surface this as a BLOCKER to the orchestrator (not improvise a deletion verb, not wire release into the stop path — that would reintroduce the SQ1 stale-F failure on every stop). Whether to add a deletion verb is a USER decision (a tracked-issue candidate), NOT this slice's scope. Until a deletion edge exists, F is retained for the process lifetime of every declared <job> — acceptable for Phase-1 single-node (the allocator is empty-on-boot, so a process restart that does not re-declare a <job> naturally drops its binding via the converge-on-boot rebuild reading the current declared set).

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design'): this step adds NO new public type and NO new allocator method — it adds the production CALL SITE that invokes the EXISTING FrontendAddrAllocator::assign (01-04) at <job> declaration. Concretely: (a) the Service arm of submit_workload (crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs, the `if matches!(intent, WorkloadIntent::Service(_))` admission block ~324) calls state.frontend_addr_allocator.assign(<job>) (the SAME shared instance on AppState, 02-01) after / alongside the service_vip allocate, fail-closing the submit on FrontendAddrExhausted (a typed error -> the handler's existing error surface); (b) a converge-on-boot rebuild fn in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dns_responder/ (or lib.rs boot path beside adopt_on_restart_recovery) enumerates declared-Service IntentKey::for_workload rows and idempotently assigns each <job>, run from run_server AFTER AppState and BEFORE the convergence-loop / responder spawn, gated by the SAME mtls_worker.is_some() block. NO new AppState field beyond the single FrontendAddrAllocator 02-01 already adds. Invent NO public surface beyond this; reuse assign/release/snapshot (01-04) verbatim — if the assigner needs a bulk-reconcile primitive the allocator does not expose, name it as a DECISION/BLOCKER, do NOT silently add an allocator method.

Depends on 01-04 (FrontendAddrAllocator — the assign/release/snapshot surface). Consumed by 01-03 (name_index frontend_for reads the binding this step writes), 02-00 (by_frontend keys on the F this step binds), 02-01 (the composition root constructs the single allocator and wires BOTH this writer and the readers into it), and 02-02 (S-DBN-WS requires F bound when the 'server' Service is declared+healthy). MUST land BEFORE 02-02 (the walking skeleton). Lands in phase 01 as a pure-Tier-1/handler-path step (no real socket); the Tier-3 end-to-end proof that the deployed Service's F is answered is 02-02 (S-DBN-WS).

RED convention: the submit_workload Service-arm assign call + the converge-on-boot rebuild fn land RED via todo!("RED scaffold: …") + the appropriate #![cfg_attr(not(test), expect(clippy::todo, …))]; dns_frontend_assigner.rs scaffolds land RED via #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]; the per-step RED->GREEN->COMMIT cycle (ADR-025) flips them, fail-for-right-reason gated by distill/red-classification.md.

Mutation gate: a per-step diff-scoped run — cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs --file <the converge-on-boot rebuild file> — achieves kill-rate >= 80%, targeting the Service-arm assign guard (a mutant that skips assign on a Service submit flips ASSIGN-01; a mutant that assigns on a Job submit flips the Service-only guard), the idempotent-resubmit path (a mutant that re-allocates on KeyExists flips ASSIGN-02), and the converge-on-boot rebuild enumeration (a mutant that skips a declared <job> flips ASSIGN-03). Note: assign/release idempotency + exhaustion are already mutation-gated at 01-04 (FRONTEND-01..04); this step's new mutable surface is the CALL SITES, not the allocator.
steps.scenario_name: S-DBN-ASSIGN-01..04 — deploy-time assign(<job>) at declaration, idempotent across resubmit, empty-on-boot converge-on-boot rebuild from declared-Service intent, single-owner writer feeds the readers' instance (release has no production trigger — BLOCKER surfaced)

phases.name: The responder + the walking-skeleton vertical slice (US-DBN-2)

steps.name: MtlsResolve re-key — by_frontend (FrontendKey=(SocketAddrV4,Proto)) translation + three-way classify + ordered drain (1b-A)
criteria: S-DBN-REKEY-01 (the frontend->live-backend translation, K-DBN-4): a proptest in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/mtls_resolve_rekey.rs proves that for every ServiceId Svc with >=1 running-AND-healthy backend, a frontend key K = (F, listener.port, Proto::Tcp) bound by_frontend[K] == Svc (and arbitrary by_addr entries), classify(orig_dst=(F, listener.port), proto=Tcp) returns Mesh AND the selected backend is the FIRST-by-Ord running-AND-healthy backend for Svc (BLOCKER-2 pinned: deterministic first-by-Ord — v1 single-replica is degenerate, but the tie-break keeps DST replay-equivalence and is mutation-gate-able) AND it is NEVER NonMesh (a frontend HIT is always mesh) and NEVER an unhealthy backend. @example: Svc=server, K=(10.98.x.y, 8080, Tcp), one healthy backend -> Mesh(that backend). The Cilium ClusterIP->backend translation made executable (research SQ4).

S-DBN-REKEY-02 (frontend hit but zero-healthy -> MeshUnreachable): a proptest in mtls_resolve_rekey.rs RELAXES REKEY-01's '>=1 healthy backend' precondition — for every Svc bound by_frontend[K]==Svc (K=(F,port,Tcp)) whose backend set is currently EMPTY or all-unhealthy, classify((F,port),Tcp) returns MeshUnreachable (the service is KNOWN — the key matched — but has no healthy backend right now: fail-closed, refuse, NO cleartext) AND NEVER Mesh AND NEVER NonMesh. Distinct from FAILCLOSED-01 (a frontend-subnet MISS); here the key HITS but the healthy set is empty.

S-DBN-REKEY-03 (the proto axis is a key field, Finding-1): a proptest in mtls_resolve_rekey.rs proves the frontend key discriminates proto — for one frontend IP F, one port P, and two DISTINCT services Svc_tcp and Svc_udp whose listeners are (F,P,Tcp) and (F,P,Udp), by_frontend holding (F,P,Tcp)->Svc_tcp AND (F,P,Udp)->Svc_udp yields Svc_tcp for key (F,P,Tcp) and Svc_udp for (F,P,Udp) (one frontend IP fronting N distinct (port,proto) listeners = N distinct entries), and a bare SocketAddrV4 (F,P) WITHOUT the proto axis CANNOT distinguish them (the collision Finding-1 names — the key MUST carry proto). The production row-producing path is proto-aware/UDP-admissible (service_map_hydrator.rs:130 + the C3 guard refusing to default to Tcp); v1 captures TCP at the worker layer so the live resolve call site keys Proto::Tcp — this Tier-1 property exercises the index's proto-discrimination at the data-structure level. A mutant that drops the Proto field collapses the two services onto one key.

S-DBN-REKEY-04 (backward-compatible additive EXTEND): a proptest in mtls_resolve_rekey.rs proves a direct backend-addr dial still resolves via by_addr — for every running-and-healthy backend addr B (in 10.99.0.0/16, a per-instance workload addr, NOT a frontend addr) indexed in by_addr (and NOT in by_frontend), classify(orig_dst=B, proto) returns the SAME verdict the pre-REV-2 by_addr path returns (Mesh for healthy B; MeshUnreachable for unhealthy B), AND a true non-mesh dst (not in by_frontend, not in by_addr, not in 10.98.0.0/16) still returns NonMesh (legitimate non-mesh egress, unchanged). The backward-compatibility guard: a mutant that routes a by_addr hit through the frontend path, or breaks the non-mesh fall-through, flips this — proving the extension did not regress the security-critical existing path.

S-DBN-FAILCLOSED-01 (the structural defense, Finding-3; K-DBN-4): a proptest in mtls_resolve_rekey.rs proves the THREE-way classify's fail-closed-on-frontend-subnet-miss arm. PROPERTY 1: for every orig_dst whose ip is a member of 10.98.0.0/16 that MISSES by_frontend AND MISSES by_addr, classify(orig_dst, proto) returns MeshUnreachable (refuse, NO cleartext — a mesh dial that arrived before the index was ready (race) OR to a withdrawn <job>) AND is NEVER NonMesh (NEVER cleartext PassThrough — the fail-OPEN regression the subnet-scoped arm exists to prevent). PROPERTY 2: for every orig_dst whose ip is NOT a member of 10.98.0.0/16 that misses both maps, classify returns NonMesh (legitimate non-mesh egress -> cleartext, by design — the live rustdoc requires a GENERAL miss stay NonMesh, mtls_resolve_adapter.rs:128-130). The two properties together are load-bearing: a mutant treating the subnet miss as NonMesh flips Property 1 (the fail-open footgun); a mutant treating EVERY miss as MeshUnreachable flips Property 2 (breaks legitimate non-mesh egress). Membership-check primitive is a DELIVER detail (the orig_dst.ip() in 10.98.0.0/16 CIDR-containment accessor — an Ipv4Net::contains on the const or a mask compare; the crafter MUST NOT invent a broader 'is this any reserved subnet' helper, REV-2 § Frontend-subnet coherence contract).

S-DBN-COHERENCE-01 (the byte-identity / single-source-of-F invariant, Finding-3 (i) — REV reconciled to the AS-IMPLEMENTED two-drains-one-allocator architecture): a proptest in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/dns_name_index.rs proves the LOAD-BEARING invariant DDN-2 actually enforces — that BOTH projections derive F from the SAME single FrontendAddrAllocator instance, so the F they key/answer is BYTE-IDENTICAL. PROPERTY 1 (byte-identity via the ONE allocator): for every batch of <job>s assign()ed into ONE FrontendAddrAllocator and driven into BOTH a re-keyed BackendIndex's by_frontend (keyed on the (F, listener.port, Proto) the allocator assigned for <job>) AND the DNS name_index (answering the SAME allocator-assigned F for <job>), for every <job> the F that name_index answers is byte-identical to the F that by_frontend is keyed on — because there is exactly ONE <job> -> F source (DDN-2 single-owner invariant); the service_backends rows supply liveness, the ONE allocator supplies which F. A mutant that introduces a SECOND <job> -> F source (a divergent allocator, a re-derivation, a stale cache) makes the two projections key/answer DIFFERENT Fs for the same <job> and flips Property 1. PROPERTY 2 (fail-closed regardless of inter-drain timing): a frontend-subnet dial (orig_dst.ip() in 10.98.0.0/16) that misses by_frontend classifies MeshUnreachable (refuse, NO cleartext) — so EVEN IF name_index exposes F for a <job> whose by_frontend key is not yet bound (the two drains are INDEPENDENT single-owner tasks — mtls_resolve_adapter.rs:55-57 and name_index.rs:41-43 — with NO temporal ordering coordination between them), the security posture holds: the dial hits classify arm-2 -> MeshUnreachable -> fail-closed, NEVER cleartext. The drain-ordering barrier is SUPERSEDED — it was an availability nicety (avoid a transient NXDOMAIN-then-refuse retry), NOT a security invariant; FAILCLOSED-01 already pins the security half. This is the in-process Tier-1 sibling of the Tier-3 S-DBN-SINGLE-SRC (02-02 oracle): both prove the answered F == the recognized F via the ONE allocator. NOTE: there is NO single shared ordered drain in 02-00 (or 02-01) — the criterion does NOT assert temporal write-ordering (which exists nowhere in the implementation); it asserts byte-identity-via-single-source (Property 1) and timing-independent fail-closed (Property 2). A mutant flipping Property 1 (second F source) OR Property 2 (subnet-miss -> NonMesh) is killed.

S-DBN-EQUIV-01 (reference-oracle equivalence + determinism — the trait-docstring contract guard, REV reconciled: BackendIndex is a SINGLE internal struct of ONE adapter, NOT a sim/host pair, so a two-implementation 'host-vs-in-memory' comparison is vacuous — x == x — and is REPLACED by an INDEPENDENT reference-oracle equivalence): a proptest in mtls_resolve_rekey.rs proves the re-keyed three-way classify trajectory over an arbitrary ordered sequence of (row-apply | frontend-binding | classify-probe) steps matches an INDEPENDENT hand-written reference oracle that re-derives the expected verdict at EVERY step WITHOUT calling BackendIndex (it folds the same step sequence directly: maintain a model by_frontend map + a model healthy-backend set per ServiceId, and for each classify-probe compute the expected verdict by the SAME three-way spec — (1) by_frontend HIT -> first-by-Ord healthy -> Mesh else MeshUnreachable; (2) miss but ip in 10.98.0.0/16 -> MeshUnreachable; (3) miss outside -> by_addr fall-through -> Mesh/MeshUnreachable/NonMesh — the oracle is the SPEC, not a second copy of the production code). For every generated step sequence, BackendIndex's verdict equals the oracle's verdict at every classify-probe (Mesh{first-by-Ord backend} / MeshUnreachable / NonMesh). This is the genuine enforcement when only ONE implementation exists: the oracle is the independent reference REKEY-01 already uses for its first-by-Ord min recompute, generalised over the whole three-way trajectory — a logic bug in classify (swap min for max, flatten an arm, drop the proto axis) DIVERGES from the oracle and flips the assertion (real independent kill power, unlike a struct compared against itself). DETERMINISM (the second clause): the same seed yields a bit-identical verdict trajectory across runs (DST replay-equivalence, testing.md § Tier 1 — the deterministic first-by-Ord tie-break is what makes this hold); a mutant introducing iteration nondeterminism (HashMap over BTreeMap, a non-deterministic tie-break) flips it. Mandated by development.md § 'Trait definitions specify behavior': the three-way arm + first-by-Ord selection IS the contract; the reference-oracle equivalence is the enforcement (NOT a two-adapter comparison — there is no second BackendIndex implementation; SimMtlsResolve is a different level, the MtlsResolve trait, and was deliberately NOT re-keyed). Exercises the BackendIndex/classify data structure in-process at Tier 1 (no real socket — the socket is irreducibly Tier 3, DDN-4).

Pinned surface ONLY (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design'): EXTEND BackendIndex (additive; mtls_resolve_adapter.rs:207, the existing by_addr/addrs_by_service maps UNCHANGED) with by_frontend: BTreeMap<FrontendKey, ServiceId> where FrontendKey = (SocketAddrV4, Proto) = (F, listener.port, listener.protocol) — the ServiceId VALUE is the row's existing content-addressed ServiceId (ServiceBackendRow.service_id), NOT a re-derivation. FrontendKey MAY be a named newtype (DELIVER detail); the CONTRACT is that the key discriminates proto. The additive classify is a THREE-way branch: (1) by_frontend HIT -> ServiceId -> first-by-Ord running-AND-healthy backend -> Mesh (else MeshUnreachable); (2) MISS but orig_dst.ip() in 10.98.0.0/16 -> MeshUnreachable (fail-closed-on-frontend-subnet-miss, NO cleartext); (3) MISS outside the subnet -> fall through to today's by_addr lookup. by_frontend derives the <job> -> F binding for its key by calling allocator.assign(<job>) on the SAME FrontendAddrAllocator instance (01-04) the 01-03 name_index answers F from — the single source of frontend truth (ADR-0072 DDN-2 single-owner invariant; run_server injects ONE Arc-shared instance into both per 02-01). RECONCILED (ADR-0072 REV-4): there is NO single shared ordered drain — the re-keyed MtlsResolve owns its OWN single-owner drain (mtls_resolve_adapter.rs:55-57) feeding by_frontend, INDEPENDENT of the name_index's OWN drain (name_index.rs:41-43); both read the ONE allocator. The temporal write-time barrier (by_frontend BEFORE name_index, the prior 'option (b)') is SUPERSEDED — it was an availability nicety, NOT a security invariant; the security half is the fail-closed-on-subnet-miss arm (2), which holds regardless of inter-drain timing. The enforced coherence is byte-identity: by_frontend MUST NOT introduce a second <job> -> F source — the F it keys on is the allocator's binding, byte-identical to the F the DNS path answers, which is what makes an answered F either HIT by_frontend (Mesh) or — if the resolve drain has not yet bound it — fail-closed (MeshUnreachable via arm (2)), NEVER cleartext. The re-keyed MtlsResolve trait-docstring contract MUST pin all three: the (F, listener.port, Proto) key type, the first-by-Ord selection rule, AND the fail-closed-on-frontend-subnet-miss arm + the equivalence test (development.md § 'Trait definitions specify behavior'). Edits mtls_resolve_adapter.rs — REV-1's DDN-1 'intercept struct provably untouched' is SUPERSEDED (the struct is extended additively, user-ratified by choosing the thin path). BLOCKER-2 RESOLVED (deterministic first-by-Ord). Invent NO surface beyond this; the v1 resolve call site keys Proto::Tcp because the worker-layer capture is TCP (mtls_intercept_worker.rs:792-794) — the future-UDP capture is the named plumbing step, NOT an index-key change. TWO REV-2 DELIVER-open details: the FrontendKey newtype-vs-tuple shape and the CIDR-membership accessor (an Ipv4Net::contains on WORKLOAD_FRONTEND_BASE) — pin as a DECISION, do not improvise.

RED convention: the mtls_resolve_adapter.rs by_frontend/classify production EXTEND lands RED via todo!("RED scaffold: …") on the new arms + the appropriate expect(clippy::todo); mtls_resolve_rekey.rs + the dns_name_index.rs COHERENCE-01 scaffold land RED via #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]; RED->GREEN->COMMIT per ADR-025, gated by distill/red-classification.md.

Mutation gate (the re-key is a primary kill target): a per-step diff-scoped run — cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/mtls_resolve_adapter.rs — achieves kill-rate >= 80%, targeting the three-way classify arms (a mutant flattening the fail-closed-on-subnet-miss arm to NonMesh flips FAILCLOSED-01 Property 1; a mutant picking last-by-Ord or an unhealthy backend flips REKEY-01; a mutant dropping the Proto from FrontendKey flips REKEY-03), the first-by-Ord selection rule, and the single-source-of-F invariant (a mutant introducing a SECOND <job> -> F source flips COHERENCE-01 Property 1; a mutant flattening the subnet-miss arm to NonMesh flips COHERENCE-01 Property 2 alongside FAILCLOSED-01).
steps.scenario_name: S-DBN-REKEY-01..04 + S-DBN-FAILCLOSED-01 + S-DBN-COHERENCE-01 + S-DBN-EQUIV-01 — by_frontend translation, three-way fail-closed classify, byte-identity-of-F-via-the-ONE-allocator + timing-independent fail-closed, reference-oracle equivalence

steps.name: DnsResponder host adapter (IP_PKTINFO socket loop, wildcard->per-addr fallback) + FrontendAddrAllocator + re-keyed MtlsResolve wired into run_server
criteria: S-DBN-BIND-01: a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/dns_responder_bind.rs (gated #![cfg(feature="integration-tests")], run via cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --features integration-tests, NEVER --no-run; is_root() SKIP per K1, uname -r recorded) proves DnsResponder::probe binds 0.0.0.0:53 (SO_REUSEADDR + IP_PKTINFO) coexisting with systemd-resolved's 127.0.0.53:53/127.0.0.54:53 specific binds (ss -ulnp confirms no EADDRINUSE), and getent from EACH of >=2 provisioned netns resolves its query — each reply source-pinned to the queried gateway via ipi_spec_dst. The acceptance SIGNAL is getent/getaddrinfo, NEVER dig @gw alone (the spike litmus: dig passes a missing source-pin, getent does not).

S-DBN-BIND-02: a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in dns_responder_bind.rs forces a wildcard 0.0.0.0:53 EADDRINUSE via a stand-in wildcard holder and proves the per-gateway-addr fallback re-derives the bound socket set from net_slot_allocator.snapshot() — one :53 socket per assigned gateway (desired == { responder_addr_for_slot(slot) : slot in snapshot }), getent from each netns resolves, AND on the converge tick a newly-assigned slot binds a new per-addr socket (add-if-missing) and a released slot drops its socket (drop-if-absent), the bound set tracking the live slot set (reconcilers.md Bar-1 converge). Example-based sad path (Mandate 11). DELIVER MAY split the fallback-fires and the converge-tracks assertions into two #[test] bodies (reviewer suggestion #2).

S-DBN-BIND-03: a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in dns_responder_bind.rs proves the Earned-Trust gate (DDN-6, wire->probe->use): Scenario A (no bindable :53 — wildcard AND every per-gateway-addr held) -> probe returns Err(DnsResponderError::Bind { addr, source }), run_server_with_obs_and_driver REFUSES boot (returns an error, process exits non-zero), and a structured health.startup.refused event names the bind failure; Scenario B (all_service_backends_rows() fails at probe) -> probe returns Err(DnsResponderError::ListSeed { reason }) and boot REFUSES with a List-failure reason. Mirrors MtlsResolve.probe() -> health.startup.refused -> MtlsBoot (lib.rs ~1904).

Pinned surface ONLY: impl DnsResponder { new(store: Arc<dyn ObservationStore>, clock: Arc<dyn Clock>, slots: veth_provisioner::NetSlotAllocator, frontend: FrontendAddrAllocator) -> Self; async fn probe(&self) -> Result<(), DnsResponderError>; async fn serve(self: Arc<Self>) } lands in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dns_responder/responder.rs (wildcard-first then per-addr fallback, the recvmsg/sendmsg IP_PKTINFO loop with ipi_spec_dst source-pin). The fourth param `frontend: FrontendAddrAllocator` is the single `<job> -> F` owner (01-04) the name_index answers F from — DISTINCT from `slots` (the per-netns reply-source-pin / per-addr fallback allocator, DDN-5, a DIFFERENT concern: keep BOTH). It is the SAME FrontendAddrAllocator instance injected into the re-keyed MtlsResolve (02-00), so the answered F is byte-identical to the F by_frontend recognizes (ADR-0072 DDN-2 single-owner invariant); the responder MUST NOT construct its own allocator or derive F independently. enum DnsResponderError { Bind { addr: SocketAddr, source: std::io::Error }, ListSeed { reason: String }, Probe { reason: String }, Socket { source: std::io::Error } } — typed thiserror, NO Internal(String), each variant -> a distinct health.startup.refused reason (development.md § 'Never flatten a typed error to Internal(String)'). Invent no surface beyond this.

Production wiring IS this step (DDN-6, no deferral; REV-2 EXTEND): EXTEND run_server_with_obs_and_driver (crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs ~1893-1957) to construct exactly ONE FrontendAddrAllocator and inject the SAME Arc-shared handle into BOTH paths — the single-owner invariant (ADR-0072 DDN-2) that makes the F the DNS path answers byte-identical to the F the resolve path recognizes. Concretely: (a) construct the single FrontendAddrAllocator (01-04) and share it on AppState beside the existing net_slot_allocator; (b) construct the re-keyed MtlsResolve (02-00) GIVEN that SAME FrontendAddrAllocator handle so its OWN single-owner drain (mtls_resolve_adapter.rs:55-57 — NOT a drain shared with name_index; the two drains are independent, ADR-0072 REV-4) derives the <job> -> F binding from it (by_frontend recognizes that F) — the probed-Ok resolve is then moved into the worker, so the worker-held resolve and the responder share the one allocator; (c) construct the DnsResponder AFTER resolve.probe() with store + config.clock + the state.net_slot_allocator handle (the DDN-5 per-addr fallback source) + the SAME FrontendAddrAllocator handle (so the name_index answers the allocator-owned F — NOT a second source), call responder.probe() (binds + seeds the name_index via List), refuse boot with health.startup.refused on bind/List failure, tokio::spawn the serve loop, and hold the JoinHandle — all gated by the SAME if mtls_worker.is_some() real-dataplane block that gates the netns/intercept path. The shipped construction site supports this: resolve is an Arc<dyn MtlsResolve> (lib.rs:1910) moved into the worker (:1937) and the allocator is Arc-shared, so one `let frontend = FrontendAddrAllocator::new()` binding can feed the resolve before the move AND survive (held on AppState) to the responder construction after AppState::new (:1944). ADD the nix socket/uio features (recvmsg/sendmsg/ControlMessage::Ipv4PacketInfo) to overdrive-control-plane + workspace nix features. The socket loop / bind / IP_PKTINFO / Earned-Trust gate are UNCHANGED from REV-1; the composition-root delta is the single FrontendAddrAllocator + its injection into BOTH the re-keyed resolve and the responder. Depends on 01-03 (name_index), 01-04 (FrontendAddrAllocator), 02-00 (re-keyed MtlsResolve).

No Tier-2 (DDN-4): the IP_PKTINFO multi-homed socket has no BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN backstop; the DST seam is the pure answer_for (01-03) + the proptested wire.rs (01-02). The socket loop is irreducibly Tier-3 real-kernel; a green check or --no-run proves nothing about runtime source-pinning.

RED convention: responder.rs production stubs via todo!("RED scaffold: …") + the dns_responder module expect(clippy::todo); Tier-3 scaffolds via #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")] (or #[ignore] only if blocked on an external resource per .claude/rules/testing.md); RED->GREEN->COMMIT per ADR-025, gated by distill/red-classification.md.

Mutation gate: a per-step diff-scoped run — cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/dns_responder/responder.rs (and the lib.rs composition diff) — achieves kill-rate >= 80%, targeting the DnsResponderError variant -> refusal-reason mapping (a mutant flattening to one reason flips BIND-03) and the run_server construct+probe+spawn (deleting the spawn RED's the walking skeleton). The Tier-3 socket arms (IP_PKTINFO, ipi_spec_dst, fallback re-derive) are killed by the Tier-3 getent litmus, not in-process mutants.
steps.scenario_name: S-DBN-BIND-01/02/03 — wildcard IP_PKTINFO bind + source-pin, per-addr fallback converge, Earned-Trust refuse-boot

steps.name: Walking skeleton — getent <job>.svc.overdrive.local resolves to a STABLE frontend F, F translates to the live backend, the hop is mTLS'd, F is stable across alloc cycles, and in-flight churn fails fast
criteria: S-DBN-WS (the production-drivable loop proof, US-DBN-2 · K-DBN-1; RE-DISTILLed to stable F; GREEN as landed 02-02 — the answered F translates to the live backend and the inter-agent hop is mTLS'd): a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/dns_responder_walking_skeleton.rs (gated #![cfg(feature="integration-tests")], run via cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --features integration-tests, NEVER --no-run; is_root() SKIP per K1, uname -r recorded; mirrors canonical_address_inbound_walking_skeleton.rs boot/deploy/netns shape) proves: GIVEN run_server_with_obs_and_driver booted in-process (real EbpfDataplane, mtls_identity_override = Some(TestPki), so mtls_worker.is_some() is active and the DnsResponder + FrontendAddrAllocator + re-keyed MtlsResolve are constructed+probed+spawned per DDN-6 + REV-2 02-00/02-01) AND a 'server' Service deployed through POST /v1/jobs reached Running-AND-HEALTHY so its <job> 'server' is bound a STABLE frontend addr F (a member of 10.98.0.0/16) and exposed by name_index, WHEN a client workload resolves 'server.svc.overdrive.local' via getaddrinfo/getent (NOT dig — K2) from inside its netns and connects to F:SERVICE_PORT, THEN getent resolves to the STABLE frontend addr F (a member of 10.98.0.0/16, NOT a per-instance backend addr in 10.99.0.0/16) within the K2 5s budget AND the connection to (F, SERVICE_PORT) is captured by the PRODUCTION-installed egress nft-TPROXY rule (BLOCKER-1 WORKS — destination-blind capture recovers orig_dst=(F,SERVICE_PORT) verbatim), the re-keyed MtlsResolve translates (F, SERVICE_PORT, Tcp) -> the current live backend, the agent's host-originated leg-B re-dial is diverted into the destination's leg-C by the REV-5 output-hook companion (mtls_intercept.rs — see feature-delta REV-5 / ADR-0072 REV-5), and the inter-agent leg-B<->leg-C hop is mTLS'd. CORRECTED TEST MODEL (RCA root-cause-analysis-dial-by-name-agent-originated-mtls-stall.md; CLAUDE.md § 'East-west mTLS tests — the egress DIALER speaks PLAINTEXT'): the dialer speaks PLAINTEXT over an ordinary TcpStream (it models an identity-unaware workload; the egress capture lands on the agent's plaintext leg-F, NOT a TLS leg) and the byte-distinct REQUEST/RESPONSE litmus proves the real server->client reply pipe. The mTLS proof is observed on the INTER-AGENT leg-B<->leg-C wire (the WireScan { plaintext: 0 } / 0x17-records oracle on lo:SERVICE_PORT, mirrored from bidirectional_walking_skeleton.rs), NOT via the client's TLS handshake — the keystone's 'client presents TLS / transitive round-trip litmus' framing is the INBOUND (prerouting->leg-C) premise and is structurally WRONG for the egress path (the captured leg has the opposite encryption role: leg-F plaintext, not leg-C TLS). The server-side held SVIDs (HeldServerIdentity) STAY — the agent's leg-B/leg-C still need them; only the test CLIENT's TLS is removed. Assert getent resolution (to F) first and separately from the inter-agent mTLS assertion (reviewer suggestion #1, keeping the K2 two-culprits message honest).

S-DBN-WS-STABLE (THE SQ1-elimination AC, US-DBN-2 · K-DBN-1; NEW — DEFERRED to overdrive-sh/overdrive#249, landed #[ignore]'d): a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in dns_responder_walking_skeleton.rs sharing the S-DBN-WS boot fixture proves the answered F is BYTE-STABLE across an alloc cycle — GIVEN getent 'server.svc.overdrive.local' resolves to stable frontend F1 (recorded) and one connect to F1 lands backend B1, WHEN the server backend is CYCLED (stopped — its AllocationId ends, its per-instance workload_addr freed — and a NEW instance starts with a NEW AllocationId -> NEW workload_addr B2, the <job> 'server' still declared) and reaches Running-AND-HEALTHY and getent is re-queried, THEN getent resolves to the SAME F1 byte-for-byte (the FrontendAddrAllocator's idempotent assign('server') returns the same F — Finding-2) AND the subsequent connect to (F1, SERVICE_PORT) lands the NEW backend B2 (the re-keyed MtlsResolve re-resolved the live backend per-connect, the churn half mtls_resolve_adapter.rs:521-540 REUSED), the inter-agent leg-B<->leg-C hop is mTLS'd (post-cycle to B2; observed on the inter-agent wire per the corrected plaintext-egress test model — see S-DBN-WS) AND at NO point did getent return a per-instance backend addr (neither B1 nor B2 in 10.99.0.0/16 was ever the resolved value — only the stable F1). One Tier-3 alloc-cycle golden example (Mandate 11); the PBT half of stability is S-DBN-FRONTEND-02 at Tier 1 (which remains GREEN and IN this slice). DEFERRED (user-approved 2026-06-28; overdrive-sh/overdrive#249): the WHEN — cycling a declared workload's backend to a NEW AllocationId/workload_addr while the <job> stays declared — is NOT achievable through the production driving ports as they stand. POST /v1/jobs/{id}/stop writes a STICKY, OVERRIDING operator-stop intent and WorkloadLifecycle DELIBERATELY refuses to schedule a replacement (ADR-0037 Amendment / WorkloadLifecycle §Bug 3); a same-spec POST /v1/jobs resubmit takes the put_if_absent->KeyExists->Unchanged path which does NOT clear the operator-stop key, and crash-restart reuses the same alloc_id/slot. NO production backend-instance-replacement verb exists; #211 is logical-workload DELETION (the opposite — release F), NOT replacement. Tracked in #249 (backend instance replacement / restart-after-stop). The AT is landed #[ignore]'d citing #249; un-ignore when #249 lands the replace/restart verb. This is the (separate-from-the-fixed-test-model) restart-after-stop scope gap surfaced and approved as a re-size.

S-DBN-SINGLE-SRC (the byte-consistency oracle, US-DBN-2 · K-DBN-4; RE-DISTILLed to the frontend->backend translation invariant D-DBN-6; GREEN as landed 02-02): a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in dns_responder_walking_skeleton.rs sharing the S-DBN-WS boot fixture proves that the addr F resolved via getaddrinfo (resolved by the PLAINTEXT egress dialer per the corrected test model — see S-DBN-WS), fed (as a SocketAddrV4 at SERVICE_PORT) into the production re-keyed MtlsResolve.resolve(F, Tcp) for the same flow, recognizes F (a by_frontend HIT — NOT a miss -> NonMesh) AND classifies it Mesh (NOT MeshUnreachable, NOT NonMesh) AND TRANSLATES F to a current running-AND-healthy backend (the Mesh verdict carries the live backend the connection mTLS-originates to) AND F is byte-identical to the addr the responder answered (one source: the name answer and the resolve translation read the SAME <job> -> F binding from the SAME FrontendAddrAllocator; the translation lands a healthy backend from the SAME service_backends rows — the REV-2 byte-consistency restatement). The intercept struct (mtls_resolve_adapter.rs) is EXTENDED additively (REV-2 supersedes REV-1's 'untouched'), read for the oracle.

S-DBN-CHURN (the active-termination posture, US-DBN-4 · K-DBN-2; NEW — NO sock_destroy; DEFERRED to overdrive-sh/overdrive#249, landed #[ignore]'d): a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in dns_responder_walking_skeleton.rs (or a churn-named file) sharing the boot fixture proves in-flight churn fails fast — GIVEN a client holding an OPEN in-flight connection through the intercept to the current backend B1 (data flowing), WHEN B1 is CYCLED mid-connection, THEN the client's in-flight connection gets a PROMPT reset/error bounded by TCP_USER_TIMEOUT (a connection-reset or timeout surfaced within the TCP_USER_TIMEOUT window) — NOT an indefinite hang AND NO sock_destroy is used (backend death surfaces through the per-connection pump task + TCP_USER_TIMEOUT/keepalive on the worker proxy legs, mtls_intercept_worker.rs:34 — the terminating-proxy posture; sock_destroy is #61 scope, NOT here) AND TCP_USER_TIMEOUT is confirmed tuned to a sane bound (the in-flight dial fails FAST, the way Cilium's own default leaves TCP socket-termination OFF, research SQ5.3) AND a SUBSEQUENT fresh connect to F lands the new live backend B2. Distinct from S-DBN-WS-STABLE: that proves the NEXT dial is live; this proves the IN-FLIGHT dial fails fast rather than hangs. One named Tier-3 example (Mandate 11). DEFERRED (user-approved 2026-06-28; overdrive-sh/overdrive#249): same blocker as S-DBN-WS-STABLE — the 'CYCLE B1 -> a NEW B2 instance while the <job> stays declared' WHEN requires the backend-instance-replacement / restart-after-stop verb that does not exist (sticky operator-stop; same-spec resubmit is Unchanged; crash-restart reuses the slot; #211 is deletion, not replacement). Tracked in #249; the AT is landed #[ignore]'d citing #249. Un-ignore when #249 lands the replace/restart verb.

Vertical-slice litmus (CLAUDE.md 'Build vertical slices through production entry points'): removing the production responder spawn in run_server (DDN-6) takes S-DBN-WS RED — getent times out (nothing answers on the gateway) and the dial never starts; removing the by_frontend translation arm (02-00) takes S-DBN-WS RED too — the connect to F misses by_frontend -> fail-closed (MeshUnreachable) -> no backend reached; removing the REV-5 output-hook companion (mtls_intercept.rs) takes S-DBN-WS RED — the agent's host-originated leg-B re-dial is never diverted into the destination leg-C, lands on the plaintext workload listener, and the inter-agent mTLS hop never establishes. NO test binds :53, installs a resolv.conf, allocates F, programs a map, or hand-installs the egress capture — production does all of those itself (the egress capture via the shipped Path-A datapath, BLOCKER-1 WORKS). CORRECTION TO THE PRIOR REUSE CLAIM (feature-delta REV-5 / RCA root-cause-analysis-dial-by-name-by-frontend-resolve-rst.md): the capture/handshake/kTLS/splice datapath is NOT reuse-verbatim for the mesh->mesh hop — the inbound interception was prerouting-only, and the dial-by-name egress hop (dialed orig_dst F in 10.98.0.0/16 != resolved backend workload_addr in 10.99.0.0/16) needs an additive OUTPUT-hook companion (the REV-5 type-route-hook-output divert + leg-C IP_FREEBIND + dual-chain teardown) so the agent's host-originated leg-B re-dial is intercepted symmetric with prerouting. This landed as REAL production code at 02-02 (mtls_intercept.rs). The dial-by-name addition over the canonical-address keystone is therefore the getaddrinfo/getent resolution STEP (name -> stable F) PLUS the re-keyed MtlsResolve translating F -> a live backend PLUS the REV-5 output-hook leg-B interception (NEW datapath surface); the inter-agent leg-B<->leg-C mTLS landing is itself the proof the resolved F was translated to the right backend (a wrong F would miss by_frontend and fail-close, never reach a backend). The two deferred ATs (S-DBN-WS-STABLE, S-DBN-CHURN) are landed #[ignore]'d to #249 and do not participate in the GREEN litmus; the live litmus is over S-DBN-WS + S-DBN-SINGLE-SRC.

getent-not-dig (the spike litmus, K2): the acceptance signal is getent ahosts/hosts <name> (or a real getaddrinfo() call) run from inside the deployed client workload's netns; a scenario asserting on dig @gw alone would PASS a broken responder (missing ipi_spec_dst source-pin) — forbidden. The K2 failure message names the two equally-likely culprits (source-pin OR healthy-gate regression).

Merge gate (ADR-0068): the Tier-3 walking-skeleton / stable-F / single-source / churn ACs assert the pinned-6.18 appliance-kernel Tier-3 matrix; dev-Lima 7.0.0-22-generic (the spike kernel) is necessary-but-not-sufficient — the IP_PKTINFO / multi-homed UDP / per-netns resolv.conf / SO_REUSEADDR-wildcard-coexistence / frontend-subnet egress-capture / TCP_USER_TIMEOUT surfaces are long-stable but MUST be re-confirmed on 6.18 (a DEVOPS/Tier-3 obligation, ADR-0072 § Design constraints inherited from the spike).

Pinned surface (RECONCILED to as-landed 02-02 — the original 'adds NO new production type' assertion did NOT hold): the walking skeleton REVEALED that the mesh->mesh egress mTLS datapath was unbuilt, so 02-02 landed REAL production code beyond driving the 01-0x/02-00/02-01 surface end-to-end. The walking-skeleton vertical-slice intent stands; the 'no new production type/surface' assertion does not. Two production surfaces landed at 02-02: (a) the REV-5 OUTPUT-hook leg-B interception datapath in crates/overdrive-worker/src/mtls_intercept.rs — the `type route hook output` divert chain + per-virt OUTPUT divert rule + leg-C IP_FREEBIND + the dual-chain (prerouting+output) teardown (new consts IP_FREEBIND + NFT_OUTPUT_CHAIN; no new typed-error variant); the contract is pinned in feature-delta REV-5 + ADR-0072 REV-5, pwru-proven on the real kernel; (b) the layer-2 workload_addr forward-carry fix in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/mod.rs — build_alloc_status_row gained a REQUIRED workload_addr: Option<Ipv4Addr> param and FinalizeFailed{Stable} now forward-carries prior_row.workload_addr (a #241-territory bug the bridge advertisement of F depended on; the residual layer-3 host_ipv4 fallback that masks a missing mesh workload_addr is tracked in #248). This step STILL drives DnsResponder::{new, probe, serve} + the run_server composition (02-01), the FrontendAddrAllocator (01-04), and the re-keyed MtlsResolve (02-00) end-to-end through the real serve+deploy path; it adds the two production surfaces above (no other new public type). The client program is the in-test PLAINTEXT dialer (per the corrected egress model — see S-DBN-WS), a staged tiny Rust bin OR a /usr/bin/python3 -c one-liner pointing at a REAL on-disk binary (K3, no phantom paths — the dns-resolver.toml collision class); listener ports avoid 5353 (systemd-resolved) and 53 (the responder).

RED convention: Tier-3 scaffolds in dns_responder_walking_skeleton.rs land via #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")] (or #[ignore] only if blocked on an external resource per .claude/rules/testing.md); RED->GREEN->COMMIT per ADR-025, gated by distill/red-classification.md. AS LANDED (re-size, 2026-06-28): S-DBN-WS + S-DBN-SINGLE-SRC are GREEN; S-DBN-WS-STABLE + S-DBN-CHURN are #[ignore]'d citing #249 (the restart-after-stop / backend-instance-replacement verb does not exist — an external production-dependency blocker, the sanctioned #[ignore] case per testing.md). DES GREEN for 02-02 is honestly recorded EXECUTED FAIL (2/4) in execution-log.json; this is the correct record of a 2-of-4 landing with two ATs deferred to #249, not a regression.

Mutation gate (RECONCILED — 02-02 DID add mutable production logic; the 'no new mutable in-process logic / vacuous pass' assertion does NOT hold): beyond driving already-mutation-gated production (answer_for 01-03, wire.rs 01-02, FrontendAddrAllocator 01-04, the re-keyed classify 02-00, responder.rs/lib.rs 02-01), 02-02 landed the REV-5 output-hook datapath (crates/overdrive-worker/src/mtls_intercept.rs) + the layer-2 workload_addr fix (crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/mod.rs). The per-PR diff-scoped mutants run on those two files (cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/mtls_intercept.rs ; and --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/mod.rs) is the gate — note the pure per_workload_rule_handles_in_dump dual-chain partition predicate is the named in-process mutation target for the teardown widening (a fixture carrying the output divert rule + the leg-S exemption proves the partition). STATUS: the mutants run is currently ENVIRONMENTALLY BLOCKED (the flaky real_tcp_probe baseline + Lima target-dir ownership, see project memory) — a MERGE-GATE to resolve before the PR lands, NOT a per-step blocker (much of mtls_intercept.rs is irreducibly Tier-3 nft/IP_FREEBIND shell-out + real-socket surface with no in-process backstop, behaviourally covered by the GREEN S-DBN-WS + S-DBN-SINGLE-SRC Tier-3 ATs). The per-PR per-package diff run (cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests) over the whole diff is the backstop.
steps.scenario_name: S-DBN-WS + S-DBN-WS-STABLE + S-DBN-SINGLE-SRC + S-DBN-CHURN — deployed workload resolves its peer's STABLE frontend name through serve+deploy, F translates to the live backend, the hop is mTLS'd, F is byte-stable across an alloc cycle, in-flight churn fails fast

steps.name: Empty-candidate honesty / NXDOMAIN end-to-end (US-DBN-4)
criteria: S-DBN-NXDOMAIN-01 (US-DBN-4 · K-DBN-2; RE-DISTILLed — withhold then resolve-to-stable-F): a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/dns_responder_nxdomain.rs (gated #![cfg(feature="integration-tests")], run via cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --features integration-tests, NEVER --no-run; is_root() SKIP per K1) proves that with a 'server' Service deployed through POST /v1/jobs but NOT yet Running-AND-HEALTHY (Pending OR Running-but-unhealthy), a deployed client querying 'server.svc.overdrive.local' via getaddrinfo/getent (NOT dig — K2) from inside its netns gets name-does-not-resolve (NXDOMAIN — the name_index WITHHOLDS the answer, no running-and-healthy backend), NO address returned (no stale/cached/unhealthy/guessed addr — NOT a stable frontend F either, since F is withheld until the <job> has a running-and-healthy backend), AND once the server reaches Running-AND-HEALTHY a re-query resolves to the stable frontend addr F (a member of 10.98.0.0/16) (the 1s SOA negative-TTL lets the retry land promptly — DDN-8). Example-based sad path (Mandate 11).

S-DBN-NXDOMAIN-02 (US-DBN-4 · K-DBN-2; RE-DISTILLed — withhold-not-release, Finding-2): a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in dns_responder_nxdomain.rs proves that after a Running-AND-HEALTHY 'server' (resolving to its stable frontend addr F via getent) is stopped through the production stop path (POST /v1/jobs/server/stop, the run_server_stop precedent at keystone:635) and converges to Terminated (poll the obs row, per the keystone's server_stopped), leaving the <job> 'server' zero-healthy but STILL DECLARED (not deleted), a deployed client re-querying 'server.svc.overdrive.local' via getent gets NXDOMAIN (the name_index WITHHELD the answer — zero running-and-healthy backends) AND NEVER the stale F's translated backend nor any stale addr (the index dropped the resolvability; no second source of liveness truth), AND (withhold-not-release): re-deploying/recovering the SAME <job> 'server' to Running-AND-HEALTHY makes getent resolve to the SAME F as before the stop (the FrontendAddrAllocator RETAINED F across the zero-healthy window — F is per-logical-workload, released ONLY on logical-workload deletion; releasing F on the transient stop would reintroduce the SQ1 stale-cached-F failure, explicitly rejected). The Tier-3 observable of the S-DBN-IDX-02 / S-DBN-FRONTEND-03 Tier-1 withhold-not-release contract. Chained narrative reusing S-DBN-WS's Given+When (one name's lifecycle). (A withdrawn/DELETED <job> would also NXDOMAIN AND release its F via the allocator's release — the deletion case is S-DBN-FRONTEND-03 Property 2 at Tier 1; this scenario exercises the transient-stop case where F is retained.)

S-DBN-NXDOMAIN-03 (US-DBN-4): a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in dns_responder_nxdomain.rs proves that with at least one unrelated server deployed and Running-AND-HEALTHY, a deployed client querying 'nonexistent.svc.overdrive.local' via getent gets NXDOMAIN AND the unrelated server's name still resolves in the same fixture (the miss does not break the hit path on the real socket).

getent-not-dig (K2): every name-path assertion is getent/getaddrinfo from inside the deployed client's netns, never dig @gw alone — the spike litmus that distinguishes a correct source-pin from a broken one.

Merge gate (ADR-0068): the Tier-3 NXDOMAIN ACs assert the pinned-6.18 appliance-kernel Tier-3 matrix; dev-Lima 7.0.0-22-generic is necessary-but-not-sufficient and the surfaces MUST be re-confirmed on 6.18 (DEVOPS/Tier-3 obligation).

Pinned surface ONLY: this step adds NO new production type — it drives the DnsResponder serve loop + answer_for NxDomain arm over the real NameIndex (the WITHHOLD seam) end-to-end through the running-and-healthy gate and the production stop path, AND observes that the FrontendAddrAllocator (01-04) RETAINS the <job> -> F binding across the transient stop (withhold-not-release; release fires only on logical-workload deletion, NOT on the stop). No API beyond the pinned 01-03/01-04/02-00/02-01 surface.

DEPENDS ON the landed 02-02 mesh->mesh egress datapath (forward note, do NOT re-scope): the NXDOMAIN-then-resolve recovery legs that DO complete a mesh dial (NXDOMAIN-01 re-query after running-and-healthy; NXDOMAIN-02 re-deploy recovery) exercise the SAME egress path 02-02 landed — they DEPEND on the REV-5 output-hook leg-B interception (mtls_intercept.rs) AND MUST use the corrected PLAINTEXT-egress test model (CLAUDE.md § 'East-west mTLS tests — the egress DIALER speaks PLAINTEXT'; the dialer speaks plaintext over a bare TcpStream, any mTLS proof is observed on the inter-agent leg-B<->leg-C wire, NEVER via a client TLS handshake). Do NOT copy the keystone's TLS-presenting dial shape onto these egress legs — that is the test-harness model error the 02-02 RCA (root-cause-analysis-dial-by-name-agent-originated-mtls-stall.md) pinned. This is a forward note for the next crafter, not a re-design of 03-01.

RED convention: Tier-3 scaffolds in dns_responder_nxdomain.rs land via #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")] (or #[ignore] only if blocked on an external resource per .claude/rules/testing.md); RED->GREEN->COMMIT per ADR-025, gated by distill/red-classification.md.

Mutation gate: the fail-honest NxDomain WITHHOLD logic is already mutation-gated at 01-03 (answer_for NxDomain arm + the NameIndex healthy-gate WITHHOLD seam) and the withhold-not-release retained-F invariant at 01-04 (FrontendAddrAllocator::release only-on-deletion, S-DBN-FRONTEND-03/IDX-02) — both at kill-rate >= 80%; this Tier-3 step adds no new mutable in-process source, so the per-step --file run is a vacuous pass and the per-PR per-package diff run is the backstop. If a small production helper IS added here, it carries its own per-step diff-scoped mutants run at kill-rate >= 80%.
