steps.name: Bidirectional ping-pong demo + EDD expectation (US-DBN-3)
criteria: S-DBN-PINGPONG (US-DBN-3 · K-DBN-3): a Tier-3 #[tokio::test] in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/dns_responder_ping_pong.rs (gated #![cfg(feature="integration-tests")], run via cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --features integration-tests, NEVER --no-run; is_root() SKIP per K1) proves: GIVEN overdrive serve booted (real dataplane) and the ping-pong bin staged at a real on-disk path (K3) AND examples/dial-by-name-responder/a.toml + b.toml exist with the [service]/[exec]/[resources]/[[listener]] schema (command -> the staged bin, listener ports avoiding 5353 and 53), WHEN Sam runs overdrive deploy a.toml then b.toml, THEN within ~15s of the second deploy A resolves 'b.svc.overdrive.local' (getaddrinfo, NOT dig) and calls B (B's counter increments, date refreshes), within ~15s B resolves 'a.svc.overdrive.local' and calls A (A's counter increments, date refreshes), each hop is intercepted + mTLS'd (TLS 1.3 records on the peer leg, observable via tcpdump / ss -tie), and both counters continue advancing on a ~10s ±5s cadence over a 60s window. Single-example golden bidirectional walkthrough (Mandate 9/11) — no PBT at Tier 3; the two [service].id's (a, b) and the ~10s cadence are the pinned example.

Fixtures: examples/dial-by-name-responder/a.toml and b.toml land (introducing the per-feature examples subdir convention, the coinflip-as-service.toml / dns-resolver.toml precedents) with command pointing at a REAL on-disk staged tiny Rust ping-pong bin (K3 — no phantom paths, the dns-resolver.toml collision class); DELIVER pins the concrete client-program form (staged Rust bin vs python3 -c) to avoid the phantom-path class (reviewer nitpick #7).

EDD graduation (.claude/rules/verification.md): this operator-surface proof graduates to verification/expectations/E05-dial-by-name-ping-pong-mtls (an O/E-surface expectation), anchored to S-DBN-PINGPONG + K-DBN-3, captured black-box against the built overdrive binary under Lima, different-fox-reviewed (never self-stamped). Like sibling E04, the capture is HONEST pending until the full-system EDD harness (#227 / #75) lands — surface that dependency, mirror E04's pending posture, do NOT fabricate a capture.
getent-not-dig (K2): both resolution legs (A->b, B->a) assert via getaddrinfo/getent, never dig @gw alone — the spike litmus.

Merge gate (ADR-0068): the Tier-3 ping-pong AC asserts the pinned-6.18 appliance-kernel Tier-3 matrix; dev-Lima 7.0.0-22-generic is necessary-but-not-sufficient and MUST be re-confirmed on 6.18 (DEVOPS/Tier-3 obligation).

Pinned surface ONLY: this step adds NO new production type — it drives the full responder + intercept loop bidirectionally through two real overdrive deploy invocations against overdrive serve. No API beyond the pinned 01/02 surface.

DEPENDS ON the landed 02-02 mesh->mesh egress datapath (forward note, do NOT re-scope): the bidirectional ping-pong (A dials b.svc.overdrive.local, B dials a.svc.overdrive.local) is TWO mesh->mesh egress hops and relies DIRECTLY on the REV-5 output-hook leg-B interception (mtls_intercept.rs) 02-02 landed. Each resolution+dial leg MUST use the corrected PLAINTEXT-egress test model (CLAUDE.md § 'East-west mTLS tests — the egress DIALER speaks PLAINTEXT'): the staged ping-pong bin / dialer speaks plaintext; the per-hop mTLS proof (TLS 1.3 records on the peer leg, via tcpdump / ss -tie) is observed on the INTER-AGENT leg-B<->leg-C wire, NEVER via a client TLS handshake. Do NOT copy the keystone's TLS-presenting dial shape onto these egress hops — that is the 02-02 RCA test-harness model error (root-cause-analysis-dial-by-name-agent-originated-mtls-stall.md). This is a forward note for the next crafter, not a re-design of 03-02.

RED convention: the Tier-3 scaffold in dns_responder_ping_pong.rs lands via #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")] (or #[ignore] only if blocked on an external resource per .claude/rules/testing.md); RED->GREEN->COMMIT per ADR-025, gated by distill/red-classification.md.

Mutation gate: EXEMPT — this step lands docs/example .toml fixtures + a staged demo bin + a Tier-3 acceptance test over already-gated production logic, with no new mutable in-process production source; per CLAUDE.md/testing.md a docs/example-only step has nothing for the per-step mutants run to mutate (vacuous pass). The per-PR per-package diff run is the backstop for any incidental logic change.

review.type: roadmap-vs-feature-artifacts fidelity review (REV-3 — design-fidelity fix; re-review PENDING)
review.summary: REV-3 closes the missing-WRITER gap found in adversarial review of the just-landed 01-03 (commit 53236c6a): REV-2 wired every READER of the FrontendAddrAllocator and the single-owner injection but named NO production actor that calls assign(<job>) at declaration, so the crafter made the DNS read path (frontend_for) the de-facto writer (allocator.assign on every query, name_index.rs:262) — order-dependent, non-deterministic, a second writer to the shared allocator. REV-3 adds 01-05 (the deploy-time lifecycle assigner: assign(<job>) at admission in the POST /v1/jobs Service arm, mirroring ServiceVipAllocator allocate at handlers.rs:324; empty-on-boot Bar-1 converge-on-boot rebuild from declared-Service intent, mirroring NetSlotAllocator adopt_on_restart_recovery at lib.rs:1980) and re-scopes 01-03 (frontend_for = PURE READER, never assign-on-read). The roadmap now carries 10 steps: 01-01/01-02 COMMITTED unchanged, 01-04 NEW (FrontendAddrAllocator surface, 1a-A), 01-05 NEW (the WRITER, REV-3), 01-03 RE-SCOPED (now a pure reader of the binding 01-05 writes), 02-00 NEW (MtlsResolve re-key, 1b-A), 02-01 EXTENDED (wires the writer + readers into ONE allocator), 02-02 RE-SCOPED, 03-01 RE-DISTILLed, 03-02 unchanged. All 43 S-DBN-* scenarios trace bidirectionally. OQ-1 remains a crafter DECISION/BLOCKER (now spanning 01-03 + 01-05). NEW BLOCKER: release(<job>) has no production trigger (no logical-workload-deletion verb exists — router is POST /v1/jobs + POST /v1/jobs/:id/stop[transient] + GET reads, lib.rs:2094-2100); surfaced for the user, NOT improvised. ADR-0072 amended (REV-3) to name the writer. Pending the Final Wave Review Gate re-run against the REV-3 spec.

carried_forward_to_deliver.title: release(<job>) has NO production trigger — there is no logical-workload-DELETION verb
carried_forward_to_deliver.location: step 01-05 (the assigner wires the assign half ONLY); the release surface IS implemented + Tier-1-tested at 01-04 (FRONTEND-03 Property 2)
carried_forward_to_deliver.status: VERIFIED (architect, REV-3): the production HTTP router exposes ONLY POST /v1/jobs (declare), POST /v1/jobs/:id/stop (TRANSIENT — the IntentKey::for_workload row PERSISTS, the <job> stays declared; handlers.rs:718-764), and GET reads (lib.rs:2094-2100). 'stop' is the withhold-not-release case (NXDOMAIN now, same F when health returns — FRONTEND-03 Property 1 / IDX-02 / NXDOMAIN-02), NOT a release. The ServiceVipAllocator precedent confirms the pattern: the VIP releases ONLY on the conflict-rollback path (handlers.rs:451-459), NEVER on stop. THEREFORE release(<job>) has no production CALL SITE to wire — wiring it into the stop path would reintroduce the SQ1 stale-cached-F failure on every stop (explicitly rejected, ADR-0072 Finding-2). The assigner ships the assign half; F is retained for the process lifetime of every declared <job> (acceptable Phase-1 single-node — the empty-on-boot rebuild reading the CURRENT declared set naturally drops a binding for a <job> not re-declared after restart). DECISION FOR THE USER (NOT this slice, NOT a unilateral action): whether to add a logical-workload-deletion verb (e.g. DELETE /v1/jobs/:id) that drives release(<job>). RESOLVED (user, 2026-06-25): the deletion-verb trigger is TRACKED in overdrive-sh/overdrive#211 ('Implement workload deletion (intent withdrawal) + service/dataplane teardown') — that verb's teardown producer will drive FrontendAddrAllocator::release(<job>) alongside its existing ReleaseServiceVip / DeregisterLocalBackend teardown (the dial-by-name frontend addr is the same class of teardown those already name). Still out of THIS slice's scope; 01-05 ships the assign half only. Until #211 lands the deletion verb this is a NAMED, scoped, evidence-grounded boundary tracked by a real issue, not a hand-wavy forward pointer.
